steps.title: Identify the causal relationship
steps.content: The passage compares nocturnal mammals: gerbils reduce activity under high moonlight (explained via cost-benefit analysis), and many others do the same. But Mohol bushbabies display the opposite pattern, and immediately the passage provides the reason: “as their heavy reliance on visual foraging results in a different balance of reward and risk.” The word “as” marks a causal explanation—the visual foraging reference directly explains why bushbabies respond oppositely.

steps.title: Match the causal explanation to the answer
steps.content: B identifies a characteristic of bushbabies that explains why they do not reduce their activity—the visual foraging reliance creates a different cost-benefit balance, so they maintain activity. The other three either add unsupported elements (A), confuse description of inconsistency with causal explanation (C), or shift focus away from bushbabies (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
